name: Chiroso

migrationPath.notes: Genetic origin confirmed as Ethiopian Landrace; exact accession unknown
How it arrived is unknown; found growing in remote farms

notableProducers: David Berrio (La Casita, Urrao, Antioquia — remote, horse-transport farm at 2100m)
William Ortiz (La Cabaña, Pitalito, Huila — recently acquired from Urrao)
description: Chiroso is an Ethiopian Landrace variety that originated in the remote farms of Urrao, Antioquia, Colombia. It has been genetically confirmed as Ethiopian in origin but the specific accession cannot be identified from the current database. How it arrived in Urrao is undocumented. The variety has only recently begun spreading south from Urrao to Huila producers — its presence in Pitalito marks a geographic expansion of the variety's cultivation. SEY Coffee has built a dedicated buying program for Chiroso in Urrao through The Coffee Quest, citing the variety's transformative impact on specialty market access for remote Antioquia producers. Cup profile: expressive tropical fruit and linden flower with exceptional sweetness.
